item.title: Posting this here in memory of my baby. I’m so heartbroken and I can’t believe he’s gone. I love you little Ezra. I’m so sad that I’m not the first thing you got to see when you first opened your eyes. I hope my sister is taking care of you.

item.title: Fresh FRIDAY daily dump for you coffee #383 (hang in there the weekend is almost here)
item.description:
1. LOTR will RUIN the mood? WTF! Divorce here now!
2. Nope, no way to to guess the guy, absolutely no way...
3. "Yeah, boss I'm working"
4. Wait, this isn't photoshop?
5. "I'm gonna eat you!" "LOL, yea sure man whatever"
6. Here is some Friday motivation for ya
7. MILK THAT AI REAL GOOD
8. This explains a lot
9. Modern problems require old school solutions
10
11. I like how he started pulling like that scene with Neo and the helicopter
12. "I'm going to rev my bike and wake up the neighborhood again tonight!"
13. Got us there, but on the other hand toast tastes so good
14
15. Thanks to the two-three people who made it to the end of this dump. When I'm not busy making questionable life choices on the internet, I've been pouring my soul into a little passion project. If you got a kick out of this dump, you're going to love my game: "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)." Think "The Stanley Parable" but with even more chaos and quirky characters. Here is my latest addition - wall signs with cake facts on them. 50% are true, 50% are made up by me. Can you guess which one this is? Can you imagine? A first-person game mixed with humor? Anyway, please wishlist: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ It would mean the world to me and my brother if you checked out the game and gave it a wishlist. It won't disappoint. I promise. Just, whatever you do, don't press the big red button!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: My bottom shelf brain is melted cheese 275/744 hour

@HileyNoteson Yoooooooooooooooooooooo
& here's my resume. Hire me.
You can find more memes at discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka
We have been currently streaming live for 275 hours to help raise money on behalf of Imgur for Extra-Life. You can catch us live for the next 20 days, 7 hours, 19 minutes, and 14 seconds on twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka
┌∩┐(ಠ_ಠ)┌∩┐you @CrimeBrulee >:(
This beautiful piece of art was made by our very own @tallandartsy who has put it up for donation for our bottom shelf community auction. All money raised by the auction will go straight to Extra-Life. Check out discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka for more information on how to donate a piece or bid on a piece ❤️
POV me trying to play anything while living under same roof as Deaf Cat Magoo (short for Oogala Magoogala) tax.

item.title: Images of Note: That Sounds illegal, I'm in
item.description:
Purity culture is the idea that women, specifically, are consumables.
EARTH: HOME OF CHALLENGE PISSING, THAT'S RIGHT, CHALLENGE PISSING!
that's basically it.
ARSON SKIRT!
And you don't seem to understand...
Whoever does this wants to erase history, and whoever wants to erase history wants to control the narrative.
To be fair, it was Will Keith Kellogg, not John Harvey, who set up Kelloggs.
If your ideology can be defeated with MDMA then maybe your ideology isn't as valid as you think it is.
The fact Quantum Computers are a thing now is kinda fucking wild.
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging

item.title: Chicago info dump
item.description:
I stole these.
Having spent a lot of time in Chicago, most are true.
Thin crust or bust!
Tamale man is the real MVP!
Fact 13 is the truth.
So many 5am bar trips. Especially if you ever spent time at The Exit. I really love Chicago and miss it dearly. Overdue for a visit. Happy Friday everyone!

item.title: “Sir, your wife just isn’t a priority right now.”
item.description:
That’s what a nurse said to Charles Johnson, husband of Kira Johnson, who was experiencing complications from a scheduled c-section. Mind you, this was a c-section that was done in 17 minutes. Most c-sections take between 40 to 60 minutes. The results of butchering a woman’s uterus to deliver her baby were rearing their ugly head. There was blood in her catheter. All of her blood tests were abnormal. • White Blood Count: 19.1 (normal range: 4-11 1000 UL) • Red Blood Count: 3.01 (normal range: 3.6-5.11) • Hemoglobin: 9.2 (normal range 12-16.0 G/dl) • Hematocrit: 27% (normal range 36-47%) Because of these results, a surgical emergency CT scan” had been ordered, so she damn well was a priority. The emergency CT scan was ordered at 4pm. Charles took this nurse aside at 9pm to ask what the holdup was on that scan. Her response was the title of this post: “Sir, your wife just isn’t a priority right now.” Around midnight, doctors finally rushed Kira into emergency surgery. The last thing she said to her husband was “Baby, I’m scared.” Another doctor tried to reassure the severely neglected couple that “It’s not a big deal, sometimes these things happen… She’ll be back in 15 minutes.” She died. When doctors went to fix the bleeding, 90% of her blood was found in her stomach. The source of the bleeding was a laceration in her bladder that wasn’t sutured properly. • Official cause of death was postpartum hemorrhage. • The REAL cause of death was being a black woman in a racist hospital. —————————————————— EDIT: There were more symptoms, but I was trying to summarize. Someone commented that these lab results wouldn’t warrant an “emergent CT”, so I wanted to follow up with these extra details: • Her fundus had raised from +2 to +4cm above the umbiculus • Her Foley catheter was draining bright red blood. • At 6pm, a bedside ultrasound showed a 6 cm (2.3 inches) heterogenous fluid collection posterior to bladder/ interior to uterus compressing the bladder suspicious for large hematoma It wasn’t until midnight until she was seen again, and at that point it was too late.
Let’s just say that Cedars-Cenai Medical Center has a habit of making their black patients “not a priority.” Surgical technician Angelique Washington testified in a 2021 lawsuit that this wasn’t an isolated incident. "I believe that some of those surgeons that was on that field, in particular, the ones that just drug out their decision making, in my heart, I felt that, is it due to her being a woman of color. “When I see the Black patients come in, I say an extra prayer. And that has been my creed of my career to this day; I say a silent prayer that all goes well. Because you do have racism very much so in the operating room.” Another doctor said this about the hospital providing lower quality of care because of structural racism: “I live that every day.” There are already many calls to action. Lawsuits have been filed, organizations have been made to prevent another tragedy like this. But while I put the hospital on blast for a moment, I didn’t tell this tale to invoke change, or as a call to action. This was far more personal. This is a letter to the nurse that Charles Johnson spoke to at 9pm, in regards to an emergency CT that was ordered at 4pm (and had not yet been done) - the nurse who said those words: “Sir, your wife just isn’t a priority right now.”
You were so ugly to the husband of a patient that you were failing miserably to care for. Kira’s death should have been a priority before it happened. The life of a new postpartum woman with a bloody catheter and abnormal blood tests wasn’t a priority because she was black and you are racist. Everyone else can take comfort in the silver lining that her death sparked awareness for the alarmingly high rate of maternal death in black women. Kira’s death did incite change. But she died because the only person fighting for her was her husband, and one of the people he was fighting against was you. Never forget that her blood isn’t just on your hands - it’s dripping off of your soul. No amount of “sensitivity training” or lawsuits, or reflection and reformation will ever un-say the words that you said that day, nor will it un-kill the patient, YOUR patient, that you were not prioritizing. . Now about the woman that you helped kill… I did some research to try to learn more about her life. Out of respect for her, what you did to her will be left out of what I found. This is celebrating her life, not focusing on who ruined (and ultimately ended) it.
Kira Dixon Johnson was incredible. She loved to travel. So much so that she was fluent in four languages. Another thing that was mentioned in another news article was that she had a pilot's license, as well as the ability to race cars. In addition to machinery, she ran races on her own two feet by running frequent marathons. She was madly in love with her husband. They were deliriously happy about their new arrival. You can tell by the photos they took before she took a turn for the worse. She loved motherhood. There’s a home video of her playing with her first son, Charles Jr. when he was a baby. She was laughing and clapping with him. She looked up into the camera with a glowing smile and said: “ "I hope this is forever. I know this is forever." Her favorite song was written by Beyoncé. Her husband didn’t specify which one in the article that I found on it, he only said this: "When Beyoncé comes on, and that's mommy's favorite song, we [him and the children] turn it all the way up to the max.” She was an amazing, intelligent, and wonderful human being. I never even knew her, and I can feel her colorful soul just radiating from these online pictures. The world is much darker without her presence, but her memory is still a glowing firefly that we can smile at.
Rest In Peace, Kira Dixon Johnson. You are never forgotten, and you’re not an afterthought or “not a priority” in anyone’s minds now. You’re certainly not in mine.
Parents’ Website Article: https://www.parents.com/pregnancy/giving-birth/stories/my-wifes-legacy-gives-a-voice-to-the-voiceless-charles-johnsons-loss-launched-a-maternal-health-revolution/ People’s Magazine article featuring an interview with her surviving husband: https://people.com/health/tk-dad-raising-sons-alone-after-wife-dies-in-childbirth/ ABC News article about the investigation into Cedars-Cenai Medical Center: https://abcnews.go.com/US/cedars-sinai-medical-center-facing-federal-probe-treatment/story?id=101165260 CNN on the 2022 Lawsuit filed against Cedars-Cenai Hospital: https://www.cnn.com/2022/05/06/us/california-civil-rights-lawsuit-cedars-sinai/index.html Today News Article: https://www.today.com/parents/parents/black-woman-died-c-section-racism-lawsuit-cedars-sinai-rcna28029 Testimony to Congress: https://docs.house.gov/meetings/IF/IF14/20180927/108724/HHRG-115-IF14-Wstate-JohnsonC-20180927.pdf
item.dc:creator: OlivetheNerd

item.title: well that sucks
item.description: For the curious, here's the original line art. It's Black prismacolor on drawing paper. ( some people did not get that the first depiction was a vampire...I drew a kind of Nosferatu version. So I redrew him as the more recognizable Dracula ) HAPPY HALLOWEEN!
item.dc:creator: JKBenton

item.title: Wisconsin Evangelical Megachurch Pastor, Gabriel Mills, Arrested. ...Again
item.description:
Arrested for using a parishioner’s phone to send himself nude photos of the parishioner’s wife. Rearrested after another survivor came forward. Don't give your phone with nudes of your wife to a pastor...is the lesson here, I think.
https://kenoshacountyeye.com/2024/10/09/journey-church-pastor-re-arrested-after-todays-court-hearing-for-more-charges-involving-second-victim/
item.dc:creator: UnattendedDeviant
